rationale: The cited paper appears highly relevant to the citing paper’s core domain: both study social influence in shared/joint hedonic consumption, and both include enjoyment outcomes (citing note: reduced enjoyment in joint consumption; cited note: congruence/incongruence changes enjoyment of shared experiences). The cited work also offers mechanisms (belongingness/accuracy) that could plausibly serve as a theoretical antecedent for the citing paper’s mechanism-focused account of interpersonal inference in joint decisions. Given this conceptual overlap and a verified DOI match, this is not safe to treat as an obvious incidental mention.
